author: .just_kk
content:
RE: Supernode behavior.

Maybe this was already discussed before. If I am not mistaken, both Prysm and Lighthouse supernodes are currently waiting for at least 64 columns to be completed before reconstructing all of them at once. I think that was implemented before cell-level deltas, when supernodes were receiving columns in full.

Rows can now reach 64-cell threshold at different times. Would it make more sense to implement row-based reconstruction to start propagating reconstructed cells sooner?

I understand supernodes rarely need to reconstruct beause they get most blobs from the EL, but this will change with blocks in blobs.

Curious to hear your opinions

cc <@106441459183423488> <@1425510242749251606> <@1000001410538151996>
